# PR Lifecycle

> 🤖 *Auto-generated by **weekly-pattern-learner** · git-commit, gh-cli, and changelog-generator cover adjacent steps but no skill orchestrates the full branch → PR → CI → review → merge loop*

End-to-end workflow for taking committed code changes to a merged pull request.

## When to Use

- "Open a PR for these changes"
- "Create a pull request"
- "Push and get this reviewed"
- "Get this merged"
- User has changes on a feature branch and wants to get them into main

Do not use this skill to commit changes — run `olko:git-commit` first if the working tree is dirty.

## Workflow

### 1. Verify branch state

```bash
git status --porcelain
git log --oneline origin/main..HEAD
```

If there are uncommitted changes, run `olko:git-commit` first.
If HEAD is on main, create a feature branch: `git checkout -b <type>/<short-description>`.

### 2. Push branch

```bash
git push -u origin HEAD
```

### 3. Create pull request

```bash
gh pr create \
  --title "<type>(<scope>): <short description>" \
  --body "$(cat <<'EOF'
## Summary

- <bullet describing what changed>

## Test plan

- [ ] <check or test command>

## Related

Closes #<issue>
EOF
)" \
  --draft
```

Title rules:
- Conventional commit format: `feat(scope): description`
- Under 70 characters, present tense, imperative mood

Open as draft first. Mark ready only after CI passes and the description is complete.

### 4. Monitor CI

```bash
gh pr checks <PR_NUMBER> --watch
```

If checks fail, run `olko:ci-debug` to diagnose and fix the failure, then re-push. Do not mark the PR ready until all required checks pass.

### 5. Mark ready and request reviewers

Once CI is green:

```bash
gh pr ready <PR_NUMBER>
gh pr edit <PR_NUMBER> --add-reviewer <reviewer>
```

### 6. Respond to review

For each review comment, address the feedback with a new commit and push to the same branch (the PR updates automatically). Reply to resolved threads:

```bash
gh pr comment <PR_NUMBER> --body "Fixed in <commit_sha>"
```

### 7. Merge

Once approved and CI is green, prefer squash merge to keep main history clean:

```bash
gh pr merge <PR_NUMBER> --squash --delete-branch
```

For hotfixes or commits that must appear individually in history:

```bash
gh pr merge <PR_NUMBER> --merge --delete-branch
```

### 8. Verify

```bash
git fetch origin
git log --oneline origin/main | head -5
```

Confirm the merge commit appears and the branch is deleted remotely.

## Git Safety

- Never force-push to main
- Never merge with failing required checks
- Never skip required reviews
- Squash merge keeps main history clean for `olko:changelog-generator`
